body:
]> 12/07/2026 7:25 15cm overnight and more on the way!.

The snowstorm arrived at Perisher yesterday afternoon and has not stopped! Since yesterday,15cm of snow has fallen and more is expected today. Check out the snowstake to see it for yourself! In addition to the natural snowfall, the snow guns were also on last night to lend a helping hand, running from 3:00am and still going at time of writing.

Today at Perisher is forecast to be cloudy with more snow showers. Winds are picking up from yesterday, with north westerly winds travelling between 33-40km/h with stronger gusts expected. Stronger winds may impact lift operations today, so be sure to download the Perisher App or keep an eye on the lift report for real-time updates.

Yesterday we saw more lifts and terrain open, including the Summit Quad Chair, bringing our current lift offering to 19 lifts. We'll continue to open more terrain as soon as it's possible and when it's safe to do so... stay tuned!

Welcome to all of our guests competing in The Scots Race today - we wish you the best of luck in your races! To our other guests, please note that The Scots Race will take place on Front Valley, so it might be a good day to check out areas like Smiggin Holes.

Off the slopes today, Jax Bar will see Jack Billman and Vibe Duo take the stage at 11:00am and 3:00pm respectively. Be sure to catch some of the performances in between runs!

The forecast is calling for more snow, so be sure to check the snow cams throughout the day and come back for tomorrow's morning snow report!

]]> weather-blank Kosciuszko Road: Chains are required between Sawpit Creek and Perisher. There are icy stretches on the road between Bullocks Flat, Skitube River and Sawpit Creek.

Guthega Road: There are icy stretches on the road between Bullocks Flat, Skitube River and Sawpit Creek. Motorists should:slow down and drive to the conditionskeep a safe distance from the vehicle in frontfollow warning signs and speed advisory signsGuthega Road - Chains are required between Kosciuszko Road intersection and the village.

Alpine Way: There is snow and ice on the road between Penderlea and Bullocks Flat, Skitube.

]]> 34.1 15 15 15 211 17 *Natural snow depth is supplemented with snowmaking in certain areas. Natural snow depth is measured at roughly 1,830m by Snowy Hydro Limited who have invested in new instrumentation across the Snowy Scheme and can now provide more frequent snow depth readings at a site close to the Spencers Creek snow course. This report reflects conditions at the date and time of this report. Conditions, lifts status and operations subsequent to this report may differ.
